title: Tinus lamponga
intro: Tinus lamponga, also known as "Gambir", is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The health benefits of Tinus lamponga include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable component in traditional medicine. The bioactive constituents of this plant include catechins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Tinus lamponga is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction and gastrointestinal issues.
